payload.text: ai-system/project-control/README.md if it exists and needs an index/reference update
ai-system/README.md if it clearly maintains a project-control document index

Add explicit Human Owner decisions for dependency satisfaction, reparenting, epic.key immutability and legacy TASK-XXX visibility.

Human Owner design decisions: hard dependencies are satisfied only by done; task reparenting is forbidden in MVP; epic.key is immutable after task refs exist; locks/resources are postponed unless required by TIG-05; legacy TASK-XXX remains resolvable during compatibility period.

payload.notes: TIG-02 reviewed and accepted. Epic key support implemented; EPIC-004 assigned TIG. Existing generated drift in CODEX_PROMPT.md and DOCS_GAPS.md accepted as pre-existing and not blocking this task.
TIG-03 reviewed and accepted. Task identity fields implemented with backward compatibility; resolver/dependencies/queue correctly deferred.
TIG-04 reviewed and accepted. Central task reference resolver implemented with TASK-XXX backward compatibility.

TIG-05 reviewed and accepted. Dependency graph, executable queue, dependency gates and generated TASK_EXECUTION_QUEUE.md implemented. Known DOCS_GAPS.md drift is pre-existing and not blocking this task.

payload.reason: TIG-06 migration depends on the dependency graph and executable queue implemented in TIG-05.

payload.notes: TIG-06 reviewed and accepted. Identity migration implemented, real project state migrated, EPIC-004 tasks mapped to TIG-01..TIG-06, generated outputs refreshed. Known DOCS_GAPS.md drift is pre-existing and not blocking this task.

payload.title: Task A - Inventory existing ctl commands and state mutation paths
Task B - Design unified control-plane architecture
Task C - Add global ID allocation strategy
Task D - Introduce ai_project_ctl core package
Task E - Add command registry
Task F - Implement unified CLI facade scripts/aictl.py
Task G - Convert old ctl scripts into compatibility wrappers
Task H - Add project doctor command
Task I - Add locking and atomic write protection
Task J - Add read-only local Web Control Center MVP
Task K - Add controlled Web write actions
Task L - Documentation and owner quickstart

payload.reason: Architecture design depends on the current ctl inventory.
ID allocation strategy depends on the current ctl inventory.
Core package implementation depends on the approved architecture design.
Core package implementation depends on the global ID allocation strategy.
Command registry depends on shared core services.
Unified CLI facade depends on command registry metadata.
Compatibility wrappers depend on the unified CLI/service facade.
Project doctor depends on the unified CLI facade.
Locking and atomic writes depend on shared core services.
Read-only Web Control Center should surface doctor health output.
Read-only Web Control Center should follow locking and atomic write protection foundation.
Controlled web writes must wait for read-only Web MVP stability.
Owner quickstart needs stable aictl interface details.
Owner quickstart should cover controlled web write action behavior after it is implemented.

payload.notes: Inventory report accepted. Findings are sufficient for control-plane architecture design.
Unified control-plane architecture accepted. Design is sufficient for ID allocation strategy task.
Global ID allocation strategy accepted. Identity, ref, UID, alias, locking, validation, and parallel allocation rules are sufficient for core package design.
ai_project_ctl core package foundation accepted. Existing ctl behavior preserved; tests and validation passed.
Command registry foundation accepted. Registry is metadata-only, existing ctl behavior preserved, tests and validation passed.
Unified aictl facade accepted. Facade delegates to existing ctl scripts, registry discovery works, existing ctl behavior preserved, tests and validation passed.
Legacy ctl compatibility wrapper migration accepted. Shared plumbing extracted safely; domain behavior, command syntax, lifecycle gates, protected-file rules, and anti-recursion boundaries preserved.
Project doctor diagnostics accepted. PASS/WARN/FAIL model implemented; hard validation delegated to owning CLIs; stale prompt/context artifacts correctly reported as warnings.
Locking and atomic write protection accepted. FileLock, atomic writes, audit append fsync, tests, and compatibility checks passed.
Read-only local Web Control Center MVP accepted. UI is loopback-only, GET/HEAD-only, generated files are labeled derived, and no web mutation paths were added.
Controlled Web write actions accepted. All writes route through aictl/registered commands, require confirmation, preserve loopback-only safety, and do not directly edit protected files.

payload.text: Human Owner approved refreshing context/Codex generated execution artifacts for CTL-12 closure only, through owning CLIs. No source behavior changes are allowed.
AI_PROJECT/state/context.json via contextctl.py/aictl.py only if the command updates it
AI_PROJECT/state/current_execution.json via codexctl.py/aictl.py only if the command updates it

payload.notes: Owner quickstart and centralized control-plane documentation accepted. aictl, web control center, doctor, controlled web writes, task refs, context/prompt flow, generated-output rules, and protected-file rules are documented.

payload.title: CTL-13 Optimize Web Control Center performance

payload.reason: Web performance optimization follows completed owner quickstart and Web Control Center implementation.

payload.notes: Web Control Center performance optimization accepted. Dashboard and data endpoints no longer run full doctor on every request; doctor refresh remains explicit; write safety preserved.

WFA-03 builds on the workflow foundation from WFA-01.

WFA-05 uses the workflow foundation from WFA-01.
WFA-05 includes linked Evolution Change acceptance helpers after WFA-02.
WFA-06 documents task workflow automation after WFA-01.
WFA-06 documents Evolution Change wizard behavior after WFA-02.
WFA-06 documents task creation wizard behavior after WFA-03.
WFA-06 documents bulk task import behavior after WFA-04.
WFA-06 documents review and close helper behavior after WFA-05.

payload.notes: Task workflow automation MVP accepted. Workflows compose governed commands, require confirmation, stop on blocking failures, and preserve owner gates.
Evolution Change Wizard accepted. It creates and prepares Change Proposals through governed commands, links tasks correctly, and preserves Human Owner approval gates..
Task Creation Wizard accepted. Create-only workflow uses governed taskctl/aictl paths, supports dependencies, does not auto-start tasks, and preserves protected-file boundaries.
Bulk Task Import UI accepted. Import is JSON-based, preview-first, validates refs before creation, requires confirmation, and creates tasks only through governed command paths.
Review and close helpers accepted. Workflows preserve Human Owner confirmation, lifecycle gates, linked Change completion rules, and reject invalid closure.

payload.notes: Tasks filtering/grouping/collapse accepted. UI now supports initiative, epic, status, search, focus section, grouping, collapsible groups, default done hiding, and readable WFA/TASK identity display.

Task row workflow buttons accepted. Tasks page now exposes Prepare for Codex, Refresh Context, and Submit for Review with status-aware visibility, confirmation previews, concise action summaries, next actions, and governed workflow routing.
